LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF YASMEEN HAMID'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES AND REQUESTS FOR PRODUCTION, AND FOR SANCTIONS. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS DISCUSSED AT THE HEARING - PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO COMPEL RESPONSES TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES 2 AND 5 IS GRANTED. DEFENDANT HAS PROVIDED NO AUTHORITY FOR ITS POSITION THAT IT DOES NOT HAVE TO PRODUCE THE CONTACT INFORMATION FOR PERCIPIENT WITNESSES. PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO COMPEL RESPONSES TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORY 15 IS GRANTED. WHILE DEFENDANT HAS PROVIDED NAMES OF SOME OF THE SUPERVISORS BUT HAS REFUSED TO DIRECTLY ANSWER QUESTIONS BEING ASKED AND PROVIDING THE NAMES AND CONTACT INFORMATION FOR THE SUPERVISORS AND EMPLOYEES THAT PLAINTIFF HAD DIRECT INTERACTIONS WITH. THIS INFORMATION IS CLEARLY RELEVANT AND THERE IS NO BASIS FOR DEFENDANT WITHHOLDING THIS INFORMATION. PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO COMPEL RESPONSES TO THE REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS IS GRANTED IN PART. DEFENDANT IS ORDERED TO PROVIDE FURTHER RESPONSES TO RFPS 1 -3 AND 13 -14. THESE ARE DIRECTLY RELATED TO PLAINTIFF AND RELEVANT TO THIS CASE. DEFENDANT MUST PROVIDE A PRIVILEGE LOG THAT SPECIFICALLY IDENTIFIES EACH DOCUMENT BEING WITHHELD ON THE GROUND OF PRIVILEGE. WELLPOINT HEALTH NETWORKS V. SUP.CRT., (1997) 59 CAL.APP.4TH 110,130. AS TO RFPS 4-5 AND 9-12, DEFENDANT IS ORDERED TO PROVIDE ALL RELEVANT DOCUMENTS IN THE THREE CATEGORIES IT HAD ORIGINALLY IDENTIFIED: (1) BLOCKED ACCOUNTS; (2) CDS; AND (3) TRAINING AND SUPERVISION OF EMPLOYEES HANDLING BLOCKED ACCOUNTS. DEFENDANT MUST PROVIDE THE ABOVE MATERIALS TO PLAINTIFF BY AUGUST 5TH, 2011. PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IS GRANTED IN THE AMOUNT OF $4000. DEFENDANT MUST REMIT THIS AMOUNT TO PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL BY AUGUST 5, 2011.PLAINTIFF TO PREPARE A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: LORETTA M. GIORGI, REPORTER: KARLA ELLIS-DAVIS, CSR #12998

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT BANK OF AMERICA, NA'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. ARGUED AND THE COURT TOOK THE MATTER UNDER SUBMISSION. JUDGE: LORETTA M. GIORGI; REPORTER: TAMARA WILLAT-HARRISON, CSR #4609 -- AFTER HEARING THE COURT RULED AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER TO THE 1ST CAUSE OF ACTION (CONVERSION) IS OVERRULED. "WHERE THE RELATIONSHIP OF DEBTOR AND CREDITOR ONLY EXISTS, CONVERSION OF THE FUNDS REPRESENTING THE INDEBTEDNESS WILL NOT LIE AGAINST THE DEBTOR, UNLESS HE HOLDS THE DEPOSIT IN A FIDUCIARY CAPACITY, AND IS BOUND TO RETURN TO THE OWNER THE IDENTICAL MONEY." WATSON V. STOCKTON MORRIS PLAN CO. (1939) 34 CAL.APP.2D 393, 403. THE RELATIONSHIP BETWEEN PLAINTIFF AND DEFENDANT IS MORE THAT THAT OF A MERE DEBTOR AND CREDITOR AND A CLAIM FOR CONVERSION HAS BEEN ADEQUATELY PLED. DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER TO THE 2ND CAUSE OF ACTION (VIOLATION OF UCL) IS OVERRULED. PLAINTIFF HAS STATED A CAUSE OF ACTION FOR VIOLATION OF THE UCL. IF PLAINTIFF CAN SHOW THAT A PROPER DEMAND WAS MADE AND DEFENDANT DID NOT RELEASE THE FUNDS, IT WAS VIOLATING A COURT ORDER AND THEREBY ACTING UNLAWFULLY. FURTHER, WHILE DEFENDANT CORRECTLY ARGUES THAT THE FAC CONTAINS NO ALLEGATIONS THAT THE ALLEGED PAST WRONGS WILL CONTINUE OR BE REPEATED AS TO PLAINTIFF, PLAINTIFF HAS PLED FACTS THAT END TO SHOW THAT THE ALLEGED PAST WRONGS MAY CONTINUE OR BE REPEATED. FINALLY, AS TO THE PREEMPTION ARGUMENT, ALTHOUGH THE DODD-FRANK WAS ENACTED IN 2011, AFTER THE EVENT WHICH GAVE RISE TO THE FAC, SINCE AN INJUNCTION CAN ONLY ALTER FUTURE ACTIONS, DODD-FRANK APPLIES AND THE INJUNCTIVE RELIEF IS NOT PREEMPTED. DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER TO THE NEGLIGENCE CAUSE OF ACTION IS OVERRULED, AND THE DEMURRER TO THE BREACH OF FIDUCIARY DUTY CAUSE OF ACTION IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. PARTIES NOTIFIED OF THE COURT'S RULING BY TELEPHONE. PLAINTIFF TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER.

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT BANK OF AMERICA, NA'S MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. ARGUED AND THE COURT TOOK THE MATTER UNDER SUBMISSION. JUDGE: LORETTA M. GIORGI; REPORTER: TAMARA WILLAT-HARRISON, CSR #4609 -- AFTER HEARING THE COURT RULED AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO STRIKE THE PRAYER FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES DENIED. PLAINTIFF HAS SUFFICIENT PLED FACTS TO SUPPORT THIS PRAYER. DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO STRIKE THE PRAYER FOR INJUNCTIVE RELIEF IS DENIED BASED ON THE REASONS ARTICULATED IN THE RULING ON THE DEMURRER. DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO STRIKE THE PRAYER FOR ATTORNEY FEES IS DENIED. PARAGRAPH 28 OF THE FAC IS SUFFICIENT TO SUPPORT THIS PRAYER FOR RELIEF. DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO STRIKE THE PRAYER FOR NON-ECONOMIC DAMAGES IS DENIED. BECAUSE THE CAUSE OF ACTION FOR CONVERSION STANDS, SO DOES THE PRAYER FOR NON-ECONOMIC DAMAGES. ERLICH V. MENZIES (1999) 21 CAL.4TH 543, 553-54. PARTIES NOTIFIED OF THE COURT'S RULING BY TELEPHONE. PLAINTIFF TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER.

MINI MINUTES:

DEFENDANT BANK OF AMERICA, N.A'S Motion To Bifurcate And Try Equitable Claims First, And Motion To Bifurcate Punitive Damages IS Off calendar. Case settled. Judge: Harold Kahn, Court Reporter: Lavena Ward, CSR #7077 =(302/HEK)

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Bifuricate And Try Equitable Claims First, And Mtn To Bifuricate Punitive Damages
Off Calendar Oct-31-2011 Continued To Nov-17-11 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-26-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT BANK OF AMERICA, NA'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT.
Appearances: Eleanor M. Roman of Severson & Werson (398.3344) for defendant and moving party; Cliff Weingus of McTernan Stender & Weingus (777.0313) for plaintiff and responding party.
Argued and the court took the matter under submission. (302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi; Reporter: Tamara Willat-Harrison, CSR #4609
---
After hearing the court ruled as follows: Defendant's Demurrer to the 1st cause of action (Conversion) is overruled. "Where the relationship of debtor and creditor only exists, conversion of the funds representing the indebtedness will not lie against the debtor, unless he holds the deposit in a fiduciary capacity, and is bound to return to the owner the identical money." Watson v. Stockton Morris Plan Co. (1939) 34 Cal.App.2d 393, 403. The relationship between Plaintiff and Defendant is more that that of a mere debtor and creditor and a claim for conversion has been adequately pled. Defendant's Demurrer to the 2nd cause of action (Violation of UCL) is overruled. Plaintiff has stated a cause of action for violation of the UCL. If Plaintiff can show that a proper demand was made and Defendant did not release the funds, it was violating a court order and thereby acting unlawfully. Further, while Defendant correctly argues that the FAC contains no allegations that the alleged past wrongs will continue or be repeated as to plaintiff, plaintiff has pled facts that end to show that the alleged past wrongs may continue or be repeated. Finally, as to the preemption argument, although the Dodd-Frank was enacted in 2011, after the event which gave rise to the FAC, since an injunction can only alter future actions, Dodd-Frank applies and the injunctive relief is not preempted. Defendant's Demurrer to the negligence cause of action is overruled, and the demurrer to the Breach of Fiduciary Duty cause of action is sustained without leave to amend. Parties notified of the court's ruling by telephone. Plaintiff to submit a proposed form of order.

MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-26-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT BANK OF AMERICA, NA'S MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT.
Appearances: Eleanor M. Roman of Severson & Werson (398.3344) for defendant and moving party; Cliff Weingus of McTernan Stender & Weingus (777.0313) for plaintiff and responding party.
Argued and the court took the matter under submission. (302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi; Reporter: Tamara Willat-Harrison, CSR #4609
---
After hearing the court ruled as follows: Defendant's Motion to Strike the prayer for punitive damages denied. Plaintiff has sufficient pled facts to support this prayer. Defendant's Motion to Strike the prayer for injunctive relief is denied based on the reasons articulated in the ruling on the Demurrer. Defendant's Motion to Strike the prayer for Attorney Fees is denied. Paragraph 28 of the FAC is sufficient to support this prayer for relief. Defendant's Motion to Strike the prayer for non-economic damages is denied. Because the cause of action for conversion stands, so does the prayer for non-economic damages. Erlich v. Menzies (1999) 21 Cal.4th 543, 553-54. Parties notified of the court's ruling by telephone. Plaintiff to submit a proposed form of order.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories And Requests For Production, And For Sanctions

MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF YASMEEN HAMID'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES AND REQUESTS FOR PRODUCTION, AND FOR SANCTIONS.

APPEARANCES: Cliff Weingus, Esq. of McTernan, Stender & Weingus 777-0313 for pltf & moving party; Eleanor M. Roman, Esq. of Severson & Werson 677-5593 for deft & opposing party.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as discussed at the hearing - Plaintiff's Motion to Compel Responses to Special Interrogatories 2 and 5 is granted. Defendant has provided no authority for its position that it does not have to produce the contact information for percipient witnesses.
Plaintiff's Motion to Compel Responses to Special Interrogatory 15 is granted. While Defendant has provided names of some of the supervisors but has refused to directly answer questions being asked and providing the names and contact information for the supervisors and employees that Plaintiff had direct inTeractions with. This information is clearly relevant and there is no basis for Defendant withholding this information.
Plaintiff's Motion to Compel Responses to the Request for Production of documents is granted in part. Defendant is ordered to provide further responses to RFPs 1 -3 and 13 -14. These are directly related to Plaintiff and relevant to this case. Defendant must provide a privilege log that specifically identifies each document being withheld on the ground of privilege. Wellpoint Health Networks v. Sup.Crt., (1997) 59 Cal.App.4th 110,130. As to RFPs 4-5 and 9-12, Defendant is ordered to provide all relevant documents in the three categories it had originally identified: (1) Blocked Accounts; (2) CDs; and (3) training and supervision of employees handLing blocked accounts. Defendant must provide the above materials to Plaintiff by August 5th, 2011. Plaintiff's Request for Sanctions is granted in the amount of $4000. Defendant must remit this amount to Plaintiff's counsel by August 5, 2011.Plaintiff to prepare a proposed form of order.(302/LMG)Judge:Loretta M. Giorgi,Reporter:Karla Ellis-Davis, CSR #12998
